title: Prior-Product Audit, Maintenance Reality, and Reframed Challenge · Claim Audit: Reframe Or Retire
summary: A successful first project has dependency drift, unresolved feedback, stale assumptions, hidden operations work, and a proposed sequel based only on more features. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp2 reframe or retire increment in maintenance audit and reframed product charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Compare repair, simplify, extend, replace, archive, and retire options against outcomes and total risk.
Measure dependency, defect, support, content, data, infrastructure, and cognitive maintenance cost before adding scope.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Reframe Or Retire · Theory Reframe Or Retire
steps.instruction:
theory case — A first product has dependency drift, support burden, unresolved accessibility feedback, stale assumptions, and pressure for a feature-rich sequel. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Compare repair, simplify, extend, replace, archive, and retire options against outcomes and total risk.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Reframe Or Retire contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Reframe Or Retire · Theory Reframe Or Retire.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare repair, simplify, extend, replace, archive, and retire options against outcomes and total risk. For Predict Reframe Or Retire · Theory Reframe Or Retire, keep Reframe Or Retire connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A first product has dependency drift, support burden, unresolved accessibility feedback, stale assumptions, and pressure for a feature-rich sequel. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Compare repair, simplify, extend, replace, archive, and retire options against outcomes and total risk.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Reframe Or Retire condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines — Controls perceivable, operable, understandable, and robust product requirements and distinguishes automated checks from human conformance evaluation.

content.text: Compare repair, simplify, extend, replace, archive, and retire options against outcomes and total risk. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A first product has dependency drift, support burden, unresolved accessibility feedback, stale assumptions, and pressure for a feature-rich sequel. The worked example variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Compare repair, simplify, extend, replace, archive, and retire options against outcomes and total risk. A defensible solution applies Reframe Or Retire, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to maintenance audit and reframed product charter.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-5
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Starting a new version is always easier than repairing the old one. This fails the Reframe Or Retire contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG 2.2 Recommendation; ISO IEC 40500:2025) — Controls perceivable, operable, understandable, and robust product requirements and distinguishes automated checks from human conformance evaluation.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare repair, simplify, extend, replace, archive, and retire options against outcomes and total risk.
